title: Reading and Use of English – Part 4
description: For questions 25 – 30, complete the second sentence so that it has a similar meaning to the first sentence, using the word given. Do not change the word given. You must use between two and five words, including the word given. Here is an example (0).

example.firstSentence: They discovered oil in this region in 1960.

questions.firstSentence: Have they repaired the broken lift yet?
questions.explanation: Tests present perfect passive ('has/have been + past participle').

questions.firstSentence: He is too nervous to give a public speech.
A hairdresser cut her hair yesterday.
questions.explanation: Tests past perfect or causative 'have something done'.
Tests phrasal verb 'give up' or passive with 'given'.

questions.firstSentence: She is more experienced than any other candidate.
questions.explanation: Complete the gap using 'most' (e.g. 'the most experienced').

questions.firstSentence: The last time I spoke to him was last month.
questions.explanation: Complete the gap using 'spoken' (e.g. 'spoken to him since').
